plan_adherence_notes: All 4 plan steps executed exactly as specified: (1) __init__ docstring added, (2) :param:/:returns: sections added to check method in RST style, (3) one-line docstrings added to cache_hits and cache_misses properties, (4) all 5 CacheInvalidationManagerTests pass. No deviations from the plan.

The questions.json served as de facto research, demonstrating the worker read the file, identified the existing RST/Sphinx style, and catalogued undocumented members. Adequate for this small-scope task but a proper research phase documenting the file's current state and the project's docstring conventions would have been more rigorous.
missed_questions: Whether the module-level docstring needed enhancement or was sufficient

reason: The execution matches the plan exactly. The docstrings added are accurate, well-written, and follow the existing RST/Sphinx style. All 5 tests pass with no regressions. The scope was small (4 docstrings added/enhanced across 3 methods and 2 properties) and the work is correct. The missed questions are minor and do not affect the quality of the delivered work.
